title: Treehouse Of Horror Xiii

jokes.text: I'm all for Halloween fun...but a seance sounds a little PG-13.
jokes.setup: Ned's characteristic over-politeness about Halloween activities
jokes.punchline: Treating a seance like a movie rating concern
jokes.explanation: Classic Ned character work - his mild-mannered approach to supernatural elements is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Chill out, dudes. It's me, Bart Simpson.
jokes.setup: Dramatic supernatural moment with 'Maude' appearing
jokes.punchline: It's just Bart pranking them with casual modern slang
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and character moment - Bart's casual attitude deflates the supernatural tension

jokes.text: Maude. You still look as pretty as the day I buried you.
jokes.setup: Ned's romantic sentiment toward his deceased wife
jokes.punchline: The morbid specificity of 'the day I buried you'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect Ned characterization - his innocent delivery makes the morbid content funnier

jokes.text: But someone made me too many pancakes...and now I have to sleep them off.
jokes.setup: Homer avoiding chores with an excuse
jokes.punchline: Treating pancake consumption like alcohol requiring recovery
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - treating food overconsumption with drunk-like consequences

jokes.text: Oh, those were for the Church breakfast. Were they now?
jokes.setup: Homer eating church pancakes
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete lack of remorse with 'Were they now?'
jokes.explanation: Homer's dismissive response shows his complete selfishness in character

jokes.text: Its webbing is a mesh of comfort and evil.
jokes.setup: Warning about the magical hammock
jokes.punchline: Mixing mundane furniture description with supernatural threat
jokes.explanation: Great juxtaposition of comfort marketing language with horror movie warnings

jokes.text: You had me at 'comfort.'
jokes.setup: Elaborate warning about evil hammock
jokes.punchline: Homer only cared about the comfort part
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - ignores all warnings for immediate gratification

jokes.text: Mm-hm. Hmm, Mr. Hammock, say hello to Madam Ass. Unh!
jokes.setup: Homer about to use the hammock
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizing his butt meeting the hammock
jokes.explanation: Juvenile but in-character humor for Homer's childish personality

jokes.text: Hmm, no bellybutton. Shuttle's in the hangar.
jokes.setup: Homer noticing clone differences
jokes.punchline: Bizarre euphemism for lack of bellybutton
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully weird metaphor that's both nonsensical and somehow apt

jokes.text: Eh, he had a good run.
jokes.setup: Clone apparently dies immediately
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual dismissal of his clone's death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Homer's characteristic lack of empathy

jokes.text: Chores? Me need clone.
jokes.setup: Clone being asked to do chores
jokes.punchline: Clone wants his own clone to avoid work
jokes.explanation: Perfect recursive laziness - even Homer's clone has Homer's work ethic

jokes.text: Outfit good, flatters butt.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if outfit makes her hips look big
jokes.punchline: Clone's caveman-speak compliment
jokes.explanation: Clone's reduced intelligence makes for awkward but honest compliments

jokes.text: Then after World War II, it got kind of quiet...till Superman challenged FDR to a race around the world.
jokes.setup: Clone telling Bart alternative history
jokes.punchline: Completely fictional comic book scenario presented as fact
jokes.explanation: Delightfully absurd alternative history that sounds like Homer logic

jokes.text: FDR beat him by a furlong. Or so the comic books would have you believe.
jokes.setup: Superman vs FDR race story
jokes.punchline: Treating comic books as propaganda with conspiracy overtones
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation adding conspiracy theory elements to absurd premise

jokes.text: Me good dad.
jokes.setup: Clone's diminished intelligence apparent throughout scene
jokes.punchline: Self-congratulatory statement in caveman speech
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - clone's self-assessment despite obvious deficiencies

jokes.text: Me not notice.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking if Dad seems dumber than usual
jokes.punchline: Clone's response proves the point while denying it
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-defeating response that confirms Lisa's suspicion

jokes.text: It would take three clones to beat the original Homer. Hmm. Huh, I mean four.
jokes.setup: Homer boasting about his fighting ability
jokes.punchline: Immediately revising upward his own assessment
jokes.explanation: Homer's ego requiring him to inflate his own prowess mid-boast

jokes.text: Yeah, but you have to leave a credit card. No problem. Discover okay?
jokes.setup: Homer requiring collateral for Ned's own chainsaw
jokes.punchline: Ned treating the absurd demand as reasonable
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity with both characters acting like this is normal

jokes.text: Twenty-three.
jokes.setup: Marge saying Homer has energy of 20 men
jokes.punchline: Homer's oddly specific correction
jokes.explanation: Homer's need to be precisely credited for his clone-powered energy

jokes.text: Now, before I abandon you in this cornfield...does anyone remember the way home? I do. Anybody else?
jokes.setup: Homer about to abandon his clones
jokes.punchline: Dismissing the one who knows the way home
jokes.explanation: Homer's selfishness ensuring his abandonment plan succeeds

jokes.text: Ah, look at that corn. Old Gil's hard work is finally paying off. Well, at least I got my health.
jokes.setup: Gil optimistic about his corn crop
jokes.punchline: Immediately losing his health to the clone horde
jokes.explanation: Classic Gil moment - optimism immediately followed by disaster

jokes.text: Like comedy clubs in the late '80s, these ravenous clones are everywhere.
jokes.setup: News report about clone invasion
jokes.punchline: Comparing to oversaturated comedy club scene
jokes.explanation: Smart cultural reference that works on multiple levels - very Simpsons

jokes.text: They've destroyed every building in town...except Moe's Tavern, which is reporting record business.
jokes.setup: Clones destroying everything
jokes.punchline: Moe's benefits from the apocalypse
jokes.explanation: Perfect logic - Homer clones would love Moe's, business perspective on disaster

jokes.text: Now, uh, who's gonna be picking up the tab? Lenny. Anything for Homers.
jokes.setup: Record business at Moe's from Homer clones
jokes.punchline: Lenny paying for all the Homers
jokes.explanation: Lenny's loyalty to Homer extended to clone army is sweet and funny

jokes.text: Currently the, uh, Vietcong...I mean, the, uh, Homers are occupying these areas.
jokes.setup: Military briefing about clone invasion
jokes.punchline: Accidentally comparing Homer clones to Vietcong
jokes.explanation: Dark humor comparing domestic sitcom character invasion to Vietnam War. Shows the absurd scale of the crisis.

jokes.text: Thank God. And you said we shouldn't let little girls in the war room. Look, I was wrong, okay?
jokes.setup: Lisa has the solution to clone problem
jokes.punchline: Official admitting sexist policy was wrong
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing official learning from mistake

jokes.text: No bellybutton? You're a clone. Then the real homer... First over cliff.
jokes.setup: Marge discovering Homer is a clone
jokes.punchline: Learning real Homer died first
jokes.explanation: Dark twist that the 'real' Homer died and this clone survived

jokes.text: My Homie's dead? How will I go on? You like back rub? Oh, well.
jokes.setup: Marge learning her husband is dead
jokes.punchline: Getting over it instantly for a back rub
jokes.explanation: Dark humor in Marge's instant acceptance and moving on

jokes.text: Lisa has a dead boyfriend. He's not my dead boyfriend. He is a dead boy who happens to be my friend.
jokes.setup: Bart teasing Lisa about Billy the Kid
jokes.punchline: Lisa's technical distinction about the relationship
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic and Lisa's precise clarification is in character

jokes.text: Or a frustrated novelist.
jokes.setup: Lisa saying Billy could have been a doctor or senator
jokes.punchline: Homer's oddly specific and pessimistic addition
jokes.explanation: Unexpected specificity and pessimism from Homer adds nice layer

jokes.text: I'm sorry. I can't live without passion.
jokes.setup: Snake giving up his gun in the amnesty
jokes.punchline: Immediately regretting peaceful life decision
jokes.explanation: Perfect Snake moment - can't give up his criminal lifestyle

jokes.text: I'm sorry. I can't live without passion.
jokes.setup: Gun ban forcing criminals to find new weapons
jokes.punchline: Snake dramatically choosing suicide over life without guns
jokes.explanation: Perfect Snake characterization - treating gun violence as artistic passion. Absurdly dramatic.

jokes.text: That's what I like about this job, you learn stuff.
jokes.setup: Snake beating up Wiggum without weapons
jokes.punchline: Lou's educational takeaway from violence
jokes.explanation: Lou's calm professionalism during chaos is character-appropriate

jokes.text: Now all I got is my enormous genitals.
jokes.setup: Moe giving up his gun that made him feel manly
jokes.punchline: TMI compensation for lost masculinity symbol
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe overshare, crude but in character

jokes.text: Now all I got is my enormous genitals.
jokes.setup: Moe talking about what made him feel like a man
jokes.punchline: Crude TMI about his anatomy
jokes.explanation: Typical Moe crude humor. In character but relies on shock value rather than wit.

jokes.text: The bumps tickle my bottom.
jokes.setup: Using former weapons as playground equipment
jokes.punchline: Ralph's innocent enjoyment of the slide
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph line - innocent and slightly inappropriate

jokes.text: Looks like the only guns left are in my cold dead hands.
jokes.setup: Billy revealing himself as villain after gun ban
jokes.punchline: Inverting famous NRA slogan while literally dead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political satire - dead outlaw literally embodying NRA slogan

jokes.text: Looks like the only guns left are in my cold dead hands.
jokes.setup: Billy the Kid revealing he's still armed
jokes.punchline: Reversing the NRA slogan while being actually dead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal of famous political slogan. The irony that he IS dead makes it perfect.

jokes.text: He ain't no cowboy. Sure I am. Yippy, wippy, wippy. Okay, he's in.
jokes.setup: Kaiser Wilhelm trying to join cowboy gang
jokes.punchline: Nonsense cowboy sounds convincing the gang
jokes.explanation: Absurd historical mashup with silly sound effects working as proof

jokes.text: Now, let's rob the bank, give the money to the poor...then rob the poor and shoot the money.
jokes.setup: Billy outlining their criminal plan
jokes.punchline: Logic deteriorating into complete nonsense
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalating absurdity ending in shooting money - classic Simpsons

jokes.text: That's piano! I said, 'pian-ee.'
jokes.setup: Moe correcting Billy's pronunciation
jokes.punchline: Billy insisting on his mispronunciation
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - outlaw's stubborn mispronunciation

jokes.text: These are horrible ghouls from the past. Hey, so are the Grammy judges. Ha-ha-ha, ugh!
jokes.setup: Marge warning about the historical villains
jokes.punchline: Homer comparing them to Grammy judges
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference that works - Grammy judges as ghouls

jokes.text: Oh, dear. Poor William Bonney might have become a doctor or a senator.
jokes.setup: Time machine causing problems
jokes.punchline: Repeating Lisa's earlier statement that started the mess
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows the circular nature of the time travel plot

jokes.text: Future Daddy needs to double kill these corpses...so they won't come back as zombies and... Get him!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to explain time travel to Bart
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical explanation ending in violence
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempt at explaining complex concepts to a child breaks down

jokes.text: Another tragedy prevented by gun violence.
jokes.setup: Homer shooting the outlaws
jokes.punchline: Treating violence as prevention rather than cause of tragedy
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire on gun debate - perfectly backwards logic

jokes.text: What is this, open mike night?
jokes.setup: Multiple time travelers appearing
jokes.punchline: Treating time travel like amateur comedy
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about the proliferation of time travelers

jokes.text: What is this, open mike night?
jokes.setup: Multiple time travelers appearing
jokes.punchline: Homer treating serious sci-fi premise like amateur comedy show
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor deflating sci-fi drama with comedy club reference, perfect Homer deflation

jokes.text: Hey, what could be more fun than an island shaped like a big smiley face?
jokes.setup: Family arriving at creepy island
jokes.punchline: Homer's naive optimism about obviously sinister place
jokes.explanation: Classic horror movie setup with Homer's obliviousness

jokes.text: Yes, completely mad. About providing topnotch vacation values. Ha-ha-ha.
jokes.setup: Family asking if Hibbert has gone mad
jokes.punchline: Redirecting 'mad' to enthusiasm about hospitality
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that maintains Hibbert's cheerful demeanor

jokes.text: Ha, ha. I've been around Scotsmen.
jokes.setup: Warning that Willie might slobber on crotches
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual acceptance based on Scottish stereotype
jokes.explanation: Relies on ethnic stereotype but fits Homer's casual prejudices

jokes.text: Well, one activity you might enjoy is not asking questions.
jokes.setup: Marge asking for activity recommendations
jokes.punchline: Hibbert's ominous non-recommendation
jokes.explanation: Good sinister turn while maintaining Hibbert's helpful tone

jokes.text: Think what Shakespeare might've accomplished...if he'd had the eyes of an eagle or could spray stink on his critics.
jokes.setup: Hibbert arguing humans shouldn't be separated from animals
jokes.punchline: Imagining Shakespeare with animal abilities
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd mashup of high culture with animal functions

jokes.text: Now, who'd like some turkey? It's a lovely nerd...I mean 'bird.'
jokes.setup: Hibbert offering dinner
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing the turkey's true nature
jokes.explanation: Classic mad scientist slip revealing horrible truth

jokes.text: 'House of pain.' This must be where you pay the bill.
jokes.setup: Marge finding ominous laboratory
jokes.punchline: Interpreting sinister name as billing department
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge logic - finding mundane explanation for horror

jokes.text: Why am I always so funny when no one's around?
jokes.setup: Marge making the billing joke to herself
jokes.punchline: Self-awareness about comedy timing
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on comedy and timing - self-aware humor

jokes.text: And I've never seen you use your tail like that.
jokes.setup: Homer describing intimate encounter with mutated Marge
jokes.punchline: Revealing Marge now has animal features
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable revelation delivered through Homer's obliviousness

jokes.text: Which I have to admit, I sort of suspected during the sex.
jokes.setup: Homer discovering Marge is now a monster
jokes.punchline: Admitting he noticed during intimacy but didn't care
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Homer - noticing something's wrong but going with it anyway

jokes.text: Hate to be a needy Neddy, but could you do me a favor? Milk me.
jokes.setup: Ned asking Homer for help as cow-human
jokes.punchline: Politely requesting to be milked
jokes.explanation: Peak Ned characterization - being polite about bizarre situation

jokes.text: Ooh, that's nice. You're actually quite gentle when you wanna be.
jokes.setup: Homer reluctantly milking Ned
jokes.punchline: Ned appreciating Homer's unexpected gentleness
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable situation made worse by Ned enjoying it

jokes.text: We prefer 'Italian-American pigs.'
jokes.setup: Homer calling them human guinea pigs
jokes.punchline: Correcting to more politically correct term
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'guinea pig' with political correctness angle

jokes.text: 'Eat my shorts'? Why, you little...
jokes.setup: Bart saying his catchphrase as animal hybrid
jokes.punchline: Homer's automatic response despite situation
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing some things never change

jokes.text: It's great. I haven't been this skinny since high school.
jokes.setup: Bart asking how Homer likes being a walrus
jokes.punchline: Homer finding positive aspect of being massive sea mammal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - walrus is 'skinny' compared to his human form

jokes.text: Makes you think.
jokes.setup: Looking at island shaped like number four
jokes.punchline: Homer's pseudo-philosophical observation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer pseudo-wisdom about something meaningless

tmdb_overview:
The 13th Treehouse of Horror episode, consisting of three self-contained segments.

Send in the Clones – Homer buys a hammock that can produce clones of himself. He creates numerous clones to help him around the house, but they soon overrun Springfield.

The Fright to Creep and Scare Harms – Lisa petitions the town to get rid of their firearms after discovering the gravestone of a young man named William Bonney who died from gun violence. However, Lisa soon discovers that William Bonney is the real name of Western outlaw Billy the Kid, whose ghost, along with the ghosts of other infamous criminals, takes over the defenseless town.

The Island of Dr. Hibbert – Dr. Hibbert invites the citizens of Springfield to his island resort, where he turns them into animals.
